mappings.rationaleAI: Aristotle's Poetics as the foundational text on literary theory, examining tragedy, mimesis, catharsis, and plot structure, directly addresses the nature of literature, literary genres, and theoretical treatises on literature.

The novel as a literary form from Defoe through modernism to postmodern forms, its relationship to print culture and the reading public, directly addresses literary genres, literature and its audience, and the history of Western literature.

Tragedy from Aeschylus through Shakespeare to modern attempts, Aristotle's Poetics on hamartia and catharsis, and whether tragedy is possible in the modern world directly address tragic literature as genre and literary history.

Biography from Plutarch through Boswell to modern celebrity biography, examining truth versus narrative and the genre's status between history and literature, directly addresses biographical and autobiographical literature.
Rhetoric from Aristotle through Cicero and Quintilian to modern revival covers the art of discourse, figures of speech, and the relation of rhetoric to grammar and literary style.
Shakespeare's Hamlet as tragedy addressing themes of death, action, and interiority, with its rich critical history, directly exemplifies dramatic literature and the history of Western literature.

Homer's Iliad as the foundational epic of Western literature, with its oral composition, heroic themes, and treatment of warfare, directly addresses the literary epic and the history of Western literature.

Tristan and Iseult as a great medieval romance exploring the conflict between passion and feudal duty, with Celtic origins and versions by Beroul and Gottfried, directly addresses the romance of love, chivalry, and adventure.
Roman satire from Horace through Juvenal, covering the distinction between gentle mockery and savage indignation and its influence on European literature, directly addresses satiric literature.
The pastoral tradition from Theocritus and Virgil through Sidney, Spenser, and Wordsworth to modern engagements directly addresses pastoral lyric and idyll forms.
Christina Rossetti's lyric poetry and Goblin Market with its allegorical dimensions directly exemplify language-based lyrics and the history of Western literature.

The Metaphysical Poets' use of conceits, paradox, and the fusion of thought and feeling, and their revival by T.S. Eliot, directly address lyric poetry technique and the history of Western literature.

mappings.episodeTitle: The Rime of the Ancient Mariner
mappings.rationaleAI: The Rime of the Ancient Mariner as a narrative poem in ballad form with Gothic and supernatural elements directly addresses poetry, the ballad form, and the history of literature.
Dylan Thomas's lyrical poetry and Under Milk Wood as a radio play exemplify poetry and dramatic literature, addressing the history of Western literature.
Walt Whitman's Leaves of Grass revolutionised poetry with free verse form and democratic vision, directly addressing poetic prosody innovations and the history of Western literature.
Alice's Adventures in Wonderland as a landmark of children's literature subverting Victorian didacticism directly addresses children's literature and fable/allegory forms.

Aesop's fables as moral and political lessons through animal characters, their transmission across literary traditions, and their role in moral education directly address fable, parable, and allegory, and folk/popular literature.
Chaucer's Canterbury Tales and innovations in metre and narrative voice directly address the novel/tale form, prosody, and the history of Western literature.
Chekhov's innovations in dramatic structure and his short stories directly address comedy/drama and the short story as literary forms.
Don Quixote as the first modern novel and its parody of chivalric romance directly addresses the novel form, satiric literature, and the history of Western literature.
Eugene Onegin as a verse novel with its innovative stanza form and blend of Romantic irony with realist observation addresses poetry, the novel, and the history of Western literature.
Biblical typology's influence on literature (Dante, Milton) and the interpretive method reading events as prefigurations directly addresses doctrinal and religious nonfictional prose literature.
Hazlitt's essays and literary criticism pioneering modern literary journalism directly address the essay, criticism as literature, and writings on literature.
Wuthering Heights as a masterpiece with innovative narrative structure, Gothic elements, and themes of nature versus civilisation addresses the novel form and the history of Western literature.

Aurora Leigh as a verse novel addressing women's creative authority, with innovative use of blank verse for contemporary subject matter, addresses poetry, the novel, and the history of Western literature.
Barbour's The Brus as the earliest major poem in Older Scots, blending verse chronicle with romance conventions, directly addresses epic and saga forms in Western literary history.
The Orkneyinga Saga chronicling the history of the Earls of Orkney and its place within the Icelandic saga tradition directly addresses the saga as a literary form.
Romanticism's literary figures (Wordsworth, Coleridge, Blake) and its emphasis on imagination and individual genius address a major period in the history of Western literature.
Aphra Behn as one of the first professional women writers, with plays (The Rover) and prose fiction (Oroonoko), addresses the novel, comedy, and the history of Western literature.
Fielding's Tom Jones with its picaresque plot, innovative narrative technique, and prefatory essays on novel theory directly addresses the novel form and the history of Western literature.
Byron's Childe Harold's Pilgrimage with its Spenserian stanza, the Byronic hero, and travelogue directly addresses poetry, lyric forms, and the history of Western literature.
Kafka's The Trial with its themes of bureaucratic oppression and the concept of 'Kafkaesque' directly addresses the novel form and the history of Western literature.
Greek and Roman love poetry from Sappho through the Roman elegists addresses lyric literature and the history of Western literature.
Shakespeare's Sonnets exploring love, beauty, time, and mortality in the English sonnet form directly address the sonnet as a lyric form and the history of Western literature.
The sensation novel of the 1860s (Collins, Braddon) with themes of crime and domestic secrets addresses the novel form and its relationship to detective fiction in Western literary history.
Sir Thomas Browne's Religio Medici and Hydriotaphia blending scientific curiosity with religious meditation directly address the essay and philosophical/scientific nonfictional prose literature.
Epistolary literature from Richardson through Rousseau and Laclos directly addresses epistolary literature as a narrative form.
Dickens's A Christmas Carol as a novella with Gothic and supernatural elements directly addresses the short story/tale form and the history of Western literature.
The origins of human literature from Mesopotamian writing systems to the Epic of Gilgamesh addresses the nature and scope of literature and primitive/folk literature.
Journey to the West as a great Chinese epic novel blending Buddhist allegory with adventure narrative addresses epic and novel forms beyond the Western tradition.
